body:
“Why aren’t my ads working anymore?” 😩

I hear this all the time.

And usually, the ads aren’t the problem.

It’s that Meta changed the rules and most people are still playing the old game.

What worked in 2023? Doesn’t work now.

The algorithm wants different things.

So I packaged everything that’s ACTUALLY working in 2026 into one bundle:

→ The Rapid Scale Framework
→ Creative system for “ugly ads” that convert
→ Retargeting playbook
→ Tracking workflows
→ Low Ticket Profits book (shipped to you)

50% Off because you’re a follower :)

body:
When I ask people how often they’re refreshing creatives, the answer is almost always the same:

“Not often enough.”

And then we look at their campaigns.

And there’s no wonder why things stopped working.

Creative fatigue is real. And it’s hitting faster than ever.

Here’s my weekly process 👇

I create between 50-100 new ads every week.

Sounds crazy, right?

But it only takes me about 10-15 minutes.

AI does the heavy lifting. Images. Copy. All of it.

Then every Monday, I launch them using my Rapid Scale Framework.

Broad audiences. Lookalikes. Interest-based. All at once.

Here’s why this matters...

If you’re noticing CPAs going up because creatives are getting stale?

You’re already too late.

You’re playing catch-up while results tank.

The goal is to NEVER get to that point.

Fresh creatives going in every single week.

So by the time one batch fatigues, the next batch is already warming up.

Most advertisers run the same 5-10 creatives for months.

Then blame the algorithm when results drop.

The ones who win?

They’re constantly feeding the machine.

New creatives. New angles. New hooks. Every week.

That’s the game now.

Say “Workshop” for the details 👇

I’m walking through my exact process, prompts, and tools next week.

linkDescription: But more importantly – I'm still in the trenches every single day, seeing what's actually working RIGHT NOW on the platform.Headline
body:
Last year, a client came to us after burning through $42,000 in ad spend with barely any results to show for it.

I asked him to show me his ad account.

What I saw made my eye twitch a little.

He was running 27 different ad sets.

TWENTY SEVEN.

Each one targeting a slightly different audience combination. Each with different placements. Each with different budget allocations based on what he "thought" would work.

Pure chaos.

His retargeting? Even worse.

He was showing the same ads to people who just discovered him AND people who had been on his sales page three times this week.

Cold prospects and hot leads all lumped together.

No wonder his CPAs were through the roof.

So I showed him a simple 3-tier campaign structure we use for retargeting.

Nothing fancy. Just a clear system that separates cold, warm, and hot traffic — and treats each group the way they should be treated.

Within two weeks, he was profitable.

Within a month, he had recouped his previous losses.

All because he stopped fighting Facebook's algorithm and started working with it.

I put together this exact retargeting structure in a simple playbook.

Takes about 10 minutes to understand and deploy.

It's called The Ultimate Retargeting Playbook.

Grab it for $7 👇

body:
I'm about to tell you something that might piss off a lot of "Facebook ads experts."

But I don't care.

Because what I'm about to share saved my business. And it might save yours too.

Here's the story...

About 18 months ago, I started noticing something strange in our ad accounts.

Campaigns that had been printing money for months suddenly stopped working.

Cost per lead doubled. Then tripled.

ROAS tanked.

And I'm not talking about small accounts.

I'm talking about campaigns spending $100K, $200K, even $500K per month.

For some of the biggest names in the industry.

These weren't amateur hour operations.

These were campaigns built by people who knew what they were doing.

And yet... they were breaking.

At first, I thought it was just a bad week.

Then a bad month.

Then I realized something much bigger was happening.

Facebook had changed. Fundamentally.

And everything I'd learned over the past decade was becoming obsolete.

See, back in the day, running profitable ads was all about targeting.

Find the right interest. Stack them together. Create 20-30 ad sets. Test like crazy.

Wait for the pixel to "learn" with 50-100 conversions.

Slowly build lookalike audiences.

That was the playbook.

And it worked. For years.

But here's what nobody was talking about...

Facebook's AI got scary smart.

Like, scary scary smart.

And all those targeting tricks we used to obsess over?

They started working AGAINST us.

Think about it like this...

Imagine you own a restaurant.

The old way of running Facebook ads was like standing at the door and only letting people in if they matched a specific profile.

"You like Italian food? Come in!"

"You follow Gordon Ramsay? Come in!"

"You're interested in fine dining? Come in!"

Sounds smart, right?

But here's the problem...

You're turning away thousands of hungry people who would've loved your food.

Just because they didn't check the right boxes.

That's what most advertisers are still doing today.

They're micro-targeting themselves into oblivion.

And Facebook is punishing them for it.

When I finally understood this, everything changed.

I stopped asking "How do I find the perfect audience?"

And started asking "How do I create ads that attract my perfect audience out of the broad market?"

Subtle shift. Massive difference.

Instead of telling Facebook exactly who to show my ads to...

I started letting Facebook's AI do what it was designed to do.

Find buyers.

My job became simple: Create compelling messages that would magnetize the right people.

Let the algorithm handle the rest.

And holy crap did it work.

Within weeks, our CPAs dropped by 30-40%.

Campaigns that were bleeding money started printing again.

Scaling became almost too easy.

But there was a catch...

This new approach required a completely different system.

Different campaign structures. Different creative strategies. Different management protocols.

Everything I'd been teaching for years needed to be rebuilt from scratch.

So that's exactly what I did.

I took everything we were learning in the trenches — managing 7-figures per month in ad spend — and compressed it into a simple framework.

Something anyone could implement in 10 minutes.

Not 10 hours. Not 10 days.

10 minutes.

I call it the Rapid Launch and Scale Framework.

Here's what makes it different:

It works WITH Facebook's new AI instead of fighting against it.

- No more obsessing over interests and lookalikes.
- No more waiting weeks for the pixel to "learn."
- No more complicated campaign structures that take forever to set up.

Just a simple system that lets you launch fast, see what's working, and scale what wins.

The framework includes what I call the "Traffic Light System."

Green = Scale it.

Yellow = Leave it alone.

Red = Kill it.

Sounds simple because it is.

But that simplicity is exactly why it works.

While other advertisers are drowning in data and second-guessing every decision...

You'll know exactly what to do with every campaign, every ad set, every creative.

No guessing. No gut feelings. Just clear actions based on real numbers.

Now, I could easily charge $500+ for this.

That's what most "gurus" would do.

But here's the thing...

I'd rather have thousands of serious marketers using this framework and getting results than selling it to a handful of people who might never implement it.

So I'm making it stupid cheap.

$5.

That's it.

Less than a fancy coffee.

Why so cheap?

Because money talks.

People who invest — even a small amount — actually use what they buy.

And I want you to use this.

I want you to implement it.

I want you to see what's possible when you stop fighting Facebook and start working with it.

Fair warning though...

There IS an upsell on the next page.

I'm also write a newsletter for advertisers and marketers where I break down exactly what's working right now. Month by month. Campaign by campaign.

You'll see that offer after you grab the framework.

No pressure to buy it. The framework stands on its own. It works. You don't have to buy anything else.

But I wanted to be upfront about it.

Because I hate when people pretend there's no backend.

There's always a backend and upsells and stuff lol.

Anyway...

If you're tired of burning money on campaigns that were designed for the "old" Facebook...

If you want a system that actually works in 2025...

If you want to launch campaigns in 10 minutes instead of 10 hours...

Grab the Rapid Launch and Scale Framework.

$5. Instant access. 30-day guarantee.

Link below 👇

I'm about to say something that might get me blacklisted from the "Facebook ads guru" community.

But I don't care.

Because this one realization saved my clients millions of dollars.

And it might save you a ton of money too.

Here it is...

Everything you learned about Facebook ads is wrong.

Not outdated.

WRONG.

Let me explain.

For years, the "experts" told us the same thing.

Create 20-30 ad sets with different interests.

Test audiences obsessively.

Wait for the pixel to learn.

Build lookalikes.

Scale slowly.

I followed this playbook religiously.

And it worked.

For a while.

Then something changed.

Campaigns that were printing money started dying.

CPAs doubled.

ROAS tanked.

And I couldn't figure out why.

So I did what any sane person would do.

I tested MORE audiences.

Got MORE granular with targeting.

Created MORE ad sets.

And things got WORSE.

I was losing my mind.

Here I was, managing ads for Grant Cardone, Frank Kern, Tony Robbins...

Some of the biggest names in the industry.

And I couldn't figure out why the old playbook wasn't working anymore.

Then I stumbled onto something.

Almost by accident.

I was setting up a campaign for a client and forgot to add any targeting.

Just broad.

Country only.

No interests.

No lookalikes.

Nothing.

I was about to fix it when I noticed something weird.

The campaign was crushing.

Like, really crushing.

Better than anything we'd launched in months.

At first I thought it was a fluke.

So I tested it again.

Same result.

And again.

Same result.

That's when it hit me.

Facebook's AI had gotten so smart that our "advanced" targeting was actually hurting us.

Every time we told Facebook "only show this to people who like Tony Robbins"...

We were telling the algorithm to IGNORE thousands of potential buyers.

It's like owning a store and only letting people in if they're wearing blue shirts.

Sure, blue shirt people might be your ideal customers.

But what about all the buyers wearing red?

Or green?

Or no shirt at all? (weird, but you get the point)

The old approach was literally blocking Facebook from finding our best customers.

Once I understood this, everything changed.

I stopped obsessing over targeting.

And started obsessing over creative.

Because here's the new game...

You don't FIND your audience anymore.

You ATTRACT them.

With messaging so compelling that your ideal customers can't help but click.

And then you let Facebook's AI do what it does best.

Find more people like them.

I've spent the last year refining this into a simple system.

Something anyone can implement in 10 minutes.

I call it the Rapid Launch and Scale Framework.

It includes everything...

How to structure campaigns for the "new" Facebook.

What to focus on (hint: it's not targeting).

The "Traffic Light System" for knowing exactly what to kill, what to keep, and what to scale.

How to scale winners without blowing up your account.

This is the exact system we use daily to manage 7-figures per month in ad spend.

And I'm making it available for $5.

Why so cheap?

Because I'd rather have thousands of serious advertisers using this and getting results...

Than sell it for $500 to a handful of people who might never implement it.

Fair warning though.

There IS an upsell on the next page.

I run a newsletter for advertisers where I break down what's working right now.

You'll see that offer after you grab the framework.

No pressure to buy it.

The framework stands completely on its own.

But I wanted to be upfront about it.

Because I hate when people pretend there's no backend.

There's always a backend lol.

Anyway...

If you're tired of burning money on campaigns designed for the "old" Facebook...

If you want a system that works WITH the algorithm instead of against it...

If you want to launch campaigns in 10 minutes instead of 10 hours...

Grab the Rapid Launch and Scale Framework.

$5.

Instant access.

30-day guarantee.

Link below 👇

linkDescription: But more importantly – I'm still in the trenches every single day, seeing what's actually working RIGHT NOW on the platform.Headline
body:
That Facebook ads strategy you learned from your favorite guru?

It was built for a platform that doesn't exist anymore.

Look, I've been running ads daily since 2012. Managed over $100 million in spend. Generated over $250 million in sales for clients like Grant Cardone, Frank Kern, Agora, Patrick Bet-David, Tony Robbins, and others.

And I can tell you with absolute certainty: The old way of running Facebook ads is dead.

Remember when we were told to:

→ Create dozens of ad sets targeting different interests
→ Wait patiently for the pixel to learn with 50-100 conversions
→ Scale slowly using lookalike audiences
→ Only try broad targeting as a "last resort"

That entire approach now works AGAINST you.

Here's why:

Facebook's AI has gotten scary smart. When you micro-target today, you're basically telling the algorithm: "Only show my ads to these specific people... and ignore everyone else who might want to buy."

It's like owning a store and only letting in people wearing blue shirts.

Sure, some of them might be buyers. But what about all the customers wearing red? Or green? Or no shirt at all? 

(Okay, maybe not that last one.)

The question most advertisers ask is: "How do I find my perfect audience?"

Wrong question.

The right question is: "How do I create an ad that pulls my perfect audience out of a broad market?"

That shift changes everything.

I've compressed years of testing and millions in ad spend into a simple framework that:

- Takes 10 minutes to set up (with your creative ready)
- Works WITH Facebook's AI instead of fighting it
- Gives you a clear "traffic light" system to know what to scale, what to tweak, and what to kill

This is the exact system we use daily to manage 7-figures in monthly ad spend.

I'm releasing it for $5.

Not because it's not valuable. But because I'd rather have thousands of serious advertisers using this and getting results than giving it away free to people who'll never open it.

Grab it below.

(BTW if you want my guide "7 Ways To Make Your Ads Work Better" just say "Seven" below and I'll DM it to you).

I'm adding some additional resources in the comments below to go deeper :)

Hope this helps!

Hernan

Hey if you're running ads, they are working and you want to scale, watch this...

(BTW, If you want my "7 Ways To Make Your Ads Work Better" guide, just say "Seven" below :)

This is a tutorial I made on how to scale campaigns using some ninja stuff like manual bidding and campaign budget optimization (CBO).

I'm also adding some additional resources to learn from me in the comments :)

Enjoy!

This is a training that I did for my free Skool community on scaling campaigns. (Skip the first couple of minutes of me rambling, welcoming people, etc lmao)

(BTW if you want my guide "7 Ways To Make Your Ads Work Better" just say "Seven" below and I'll DM it to you).

If your ads are working well but you want them to work better and scale them, this will help...

I'm adding a link to join the community (and some other resources) in the comments below.

Hope this helps!

Hernan

Alex Hormozi's Launch Just Proved That Targeting Is Dead (And How To Use This 2-Campaign Strategy To SCALE)

Alright I've been beating this drum for a while now...

But Hormozi's launch just proven that things have shifted BIG TIME with ads and targeting

On this post, I'll show you how you can leverage this too...

It's not a new targeting strategy. It's not a budget optimization hack. It's not even a funnel tweak.

While most advertisers are still obsessing over audience, demographics, psychographics, etc...

I've built a two-campaign system that lets Facebook do what it does best - find buyers - while I focus on what actually moves the needle - creating ads that convert.

Here's the framework that's scaling accounts faster and more predictably than anything I've used before.

=== Targeting Is Dead (And That's Good News)

Hormozi's launch was crazy and everyone is talking about it...

And it goes to show you how much Meta Ads have changed over the past 6-7 months...

Think about it - when was the last time perfect targeting saved a crappy ad? Never.

But I've seen amazing creative work with terrible targeting all the time.

In fact, you could say that my targeting is pretty terrible... but still CPAs are amazing!

Going back to Hormozi's launch...

His team created thousands of creatives. Thousands.

Then they basically said "Facebook, target everyone" and let the platform do its thing.

I mean, it helped big time that they've been building this humongous audience with tons of good will and content...

But it was a creative-heavy approach...

So here's the new playbook: tons of creative + let Facebook run wild = massive scale.

=== Your Only Job in 2025

Here's what I'm actually doing these days:

Find new creatives. Every week.

That's it.

Not audience research. Not demographic deep-dives. Not interest rabbit holes.

Creative creation and creative testing.

Facebook finds buyers. You make stuff worth buying.

Instead of asking "who should I target?" ask "what will make someone stop scrolling?"

Instead of optimizing audiences, optimize hooks.

=== The 2-Campaign System That Actually Works

After months of testing, here's the simple structure that's working:

Campaign 1: Creative Testing

Find winning creative.

Campaign 2: Scaling Machine

Take winners and scale them with zero limits.

The magic is how these work together.

== Campaign 1: Creative Testing

This is where you test new stuff every week.

Setup:

- Conversion optimized campaign.
- Broad targeting/Interest/Lookalikes - All Advantage +

Weekly Process:

- Monday: Launch 3-5 new creative variations
- Wednesday: Kill the losers
- Friday: Find potential winners

What You Test:

- New angles and hooks
- Different formats (video, carousel, images)
- Different messaging
- Different calls to action

Goal: Find what works. Not scale yet.

= Campaign 2: Scaling Machine

This is where winners go to make money.

Setup:

- Advantage+ Sales Campaign (or completely broad)
- Zero demographic limits
- Zero interest targeting
- Higher budget (2-3x your testing budget)
- Only proven creative from Campaign 1 (creatives with 5-10 buyers/leads/sales at a good KPI)

How It Works:

Facebook gets total freedom to find buyers however it wants.

When cold traffic gets expensive, it goes to warm audiences. When warm audiences get saturated, it finds new cold people.

You don't babysit this campaign. You feed it winners and let it run.

=== How to Move Winners from Testing to Scaling

Every 2-3 weeks, check Campaign 1 performance.

Any creative hitting your targets gets moved to Campaign 2 using its post ID.

- What You Look For:

Hitting target cost per acquisition
Working for at least a week
Good engagement
Converting consistently

Once it's in Campaign 2, it can run for months without touching it.

=== Why Facebook Sucks at Creative

Facebook keeps trying to automate creative with AI.

The results are usually awful.

I've seen it add weird AI smiles to photos, write terrible copy, and create combinations that make no sense.

Facebook's creative AI sucks. But their targeting AI is amazing.

Perfect: you handle creative, they handle targeting.

=== he Creative Volume Reality

You need way more creative than you think.

Most people launch 2-3 ads and expect them to work forever.

That's not how it works anymore.

Facebook punishes old creative unless you give it complete targeting freedom. Fresh creative gets lower costs and better reach.

My weekly schedule:

Monday: Launch 3-5 new angles
Wednesday: Kill underperformers
Friday: Plan next week based on data
Every 2-3 weeks: Move winners to scaling

This creates a constant flow of fresh creative while scaling what works.

And Facebook keeps on taking targeting options away from advertisers... I think that's actually good news.

But they mess around with creatives too much. That's not that cool. Turn that crap off lol

The Bottom Line

Great creative + Facebook freedom = scale.

Make ads people want to watch. Let Facebook find those people.

The 2-campaign system gives you both - systematic testing and automated scaling.

Most importantly, it works with how Facebook actually operates instead of against it.

Your targeting research won't save bad creative. But great creative beats bad targeting every time.

And of course, you need a good offer, that goes without saying lol

Time to stop playing audience detective and start making stuff people want to buy.

Hernan

P.S: Want more support? Go to RapidScaleFramework.com/apply

linkDescription: The Secret Framework The Big Dogs Use To Get PAID To Build Their List
body:
Your lead magnet is bleeding you dry.

You're paying $3-$10 per lead. Maybe more.

Then you're trying to nurture them for weeks before asking for money.

Half don't open your emails. Most never buy.

Here's what changed my entire approach:

Stop giving away free stuff. Start selling cheap stuff.

Low-ticket offers ($5-$27) do three things free lead magnets can't:

→ They cover your ad costs (sometimes exceed them)
→ They attract buyers, not freebie seekers
→ They prove your product works before you ask for big money

I wrote a book on this exact system. The same one I used to manage $100M+ in ad spend for big industry names...
